speaker: YORK
text: From Ireland thus comes York to claim his right

speaker: YORK
text: And pluck the crown from feeble Henry’s head.

speaker: YORK
text: Ring, bells, aloud! Burn, bonfires, clear and bright

speaker: YORK
text: To entertain great England’s lawful king!

speaker: YORK
text: Ah, sancta maiestas, who would not buy thee dear?

speaker: YORK
text: Let them obey that knows not how to rule.

speaker: YORK
text: This hand was made to handle naught but gold.

speaker: YORK
text: I cannot give due action to my words

speaker: YORK
text: Except a sword or scepter balance it.

speaker: YORK
text: A scepter shall it have, have I a soul,

speaker: YORK
text: On which I’ll toss the fleur-de-luce of France.

speaker: YORK
text:
Aside
                .
                 
              Whom have we here? Buckingham, to disturb me?

speaker: YORK
text: The King hath sent him, sure. I must dissemble.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: York, if thou meanest well, I greet thee well.

speaker: YORK
text: Humphrey of Buckingham, I accept thy greeting.

speaker: YORK
text: Art thou a messenger, or come of pleasure?

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: A messenger from Henry, our dread liege,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: To know the reason of these arms in peace;

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Or why thou, being a subject as I am,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Against thy oath and true allegiance sworn,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Should raise so great a power without his leave,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Or dare to bring thy force so near the court.

speaker: YORK
text: Scarce can I speak, my choler is so great.

speaker: YORK
text: O, I could hew up rocks and fight with flint,

speaker: YORK
text: I am so angry at these abject terms!

speaker: YORK
text: And now, like Ajax Telamonius,

speaker: YORK
text: On sheep or oxen could I spend my fury.

speaker: YORK
text: I am far better born than is the King,

speaker: YORK
text: More like a king, more kingly in my thoughts.

speaker: YORK
text: But I must make fair weather yet awhile,

speaker: YORK
text: Till Henry be more weak and I more strong.—

speaker: YORK
text: Buckingham, I prithee, pardon me,

speaker: YORK
text: That I have given no answer all this while.

speaker: YORK
text: My mind was troubled with deep melancholy.

speaker: YORK
text: The cause why I have brought this army hither

speaker: YORK
text: Is to remove proud Somerset from the King,

speaker: YORK
text: Seditious to his Grace and to the state.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: That is too much presumption on thy part.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: But if thy arms be to no other end,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: The King hath yielded unto thy demand:

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: The Duke of Somerset is in the Tower.

speaker: YORK
text: Upon thine honor, is he prisoner?

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Upon mine honor, he is prisoner.

speaker: YORK
text: Then, Buckingham, I do dismiss my powers.—

speaker: YORK
text: Soldiers, I thank you all. Disperse yourselves.

speaker: YORK
text: Meet me tomorrow in Saint George’s field;

speaker: YORK
text: You shall have pay and everything you wish.

speaker: YORK
text: And let my sovereign, virtuous Henry,

speaker: YORK
text: Command my eldest son, nay, all my sons,

speaker: YORK
text: As pledges of my fealty and love;

speaker: YORK
text: I’ll send them all as willing as I live.

speaker: YORK
text: Lands, goods, horse, armor, anything I have

speaker: YORK
text: Is his to use, so Somerset may die.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: York, I commend this kind submission.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: We twain will go into his Highness’ tent.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Buckingham, doth York intend no harm to us

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That thus he marcheth with thee arm in arm?

speaker: YORK
text: In all submission and humility

speaker: YORK
text: York doth present himself unto your Highness.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Then what intends these forces thou dost bring?

speaker: YORK
text: To heave the traitor Somerset from hence

speaker: YORK
text: And fight against that monstrous rebel Cade,

speaker: YORK
text: Who since I heard to be discomfited.

speaker: IDEN
text: If one so rude and of so mean condition

speaker: IDEN
text: May pass into the presence of a king,

speaker: IDEN
text: Lo, I present your Grace a traitor’s head,

speaker: IDEN
text: The head of Cade, whom I in combat slew.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The head of Cade? Great God, how just art Thou!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: O, let me view his visage, being dead,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That living wrought me such exceeding trouble.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Tell me, my friend, art thou the man that slew him?

speaker: IDEN
text: I was, an ’t like your Majesty.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: How art thou called? And what is thy degree?

speaker: IDEN
text: Alexander Iden, that’s my name,

speaker: IDEN
text: A poor esquire of Kent that loves his king.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: So please it you, my lord, ’twere not amiss

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: He were created knight for his good service.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text:
Iden, kneel down.
                 
                He
                 
                kneels
                .
               Rise up a knight.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: We give thee for reward a thousand marks,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And will that thou henceforth attend on us.

speaker: IDEN
text: May Iden live to merit such a bounty,

speaker: IDEN
text: And never live but true unto his liege!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: See, Buckingham, Somerset comes with th’ Queen.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Go bid her hide him quickly from the Duke.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: For thousand Yorks he shall not hide his head,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: But boldly stand and front him to his face.

speaker: YORK
text: How now? Is Somerset at liberty?

speaker: YORK
text: Then, York, unloose thy long-imprisoned thoughts,

speaker: YORK
text: And let thy tongue be equal with thy heart.

speaker: YORK
text: Shall I endure the sight of Somerset?—

speaker: YORK
text: False king, why hast thou broken faith with me,

speaker: YORK
text: Knowing how hardly I can brook abuse?

speaker: YORK
text: “King” did I call thee? No, thou art not king,

speaker: YORK
text: Not fit to govern and rule multitudes,

speaker: YORK
text: Which dar’st not—no, nor canst not—rule a traitor.

speaker: YORK
text: That head of thine doth not become a crown;

speaker: YORK
text: Thy hand is made to grasp a palmer’s staff,

speaker: YORK
text: And not to grace an awful princely scepter.

speaker: YORK
text: That gold must round engirt these brows of mine,

speaker: YORK
text: Whose smile and frown, like to Achilles’ spear,

speaker: YORK
text: Is able with the change to kill and cure.

speaker: YORK
text: Here is a hand to hold a scepter up

speaker: YORK
text: And with the same to act controlling laws.

speaker: YORK
text: Give place. By heaven, thou shalt rule no more

speaker: YORK
text: O’er him whom heaven created for thy ruler.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: O monstrous traitor! I arrest thee, York,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Of capital treason ’gainst the King and crown.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Obey, audacious traitor. Kneel for grace.

speaker: YORK
text: Wouldst have me kneel? First let me ask of these

speaker: YORK
text: If they can brook I bow a knee to man.

speaker: YORK
text:
To
                 
                an
                 
                Attendant
                .
                 
              Sirrah, call in my sons to be my bail.

speaker: YORK
text: I know, ere they will have me go to ward,

speaker: YORK
text: They’ll pawn their swords for my enfranchisement.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Call hither Clifford; bid him come amain,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: To say if that the bastard boys of York

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Shall be the surety for their traitor father.

speaker: YORK
text: O, blood-bespotted Neapolitan,

speaker: YORK
text: Outcast of Naples, England’s bloody scourge!

speaker: YORK
text: The sons of York, thy betters in their birth,

speaker: YORK
text: Shall be their father’s bail, and bane to those

speaker: YORK
text: That for my surety will refuse the boys.

speaker: YORK
text: See where they come; I’ll warrant they’ll make it good.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And here comes Clifford to deny their bail.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Health and all happiness to my lord the King.

speaker: YORK
text: I thank thee, Clifford. Say, what news with thee?

speaker: YORK
text: Nay, do not fright us with an angry look.

speaker: YORK
text: We are thy sovereign, Clifford; kneel again.

speaker: YORK
text: For thy mistaking so, we pardon thee.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: This is my king, York; I do not mistake,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: But thou mistakes me much to think I do.—

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: To Bedlam with him! Is the man grown mad?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Ay, Clifford, a bedlam and ambitious humor

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Makes him oppose himself against his king.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: He is a traitor. Let him to the Tower,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: And chop away that factious pate of his.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: He is arrested, but will not obey.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: His sons, he says, shall give their words for him.

speaker: YORK
text: Will you not, sons?

speaker: EDWARD
text: Ay, noble father, if our words will serve.

speaker: RICHARD
text: And if words will not, then our weapons shall.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Why, what a brood of traitors have we here!

speaker: YORK
text: Look in a glass, and call thy image so.

speaker: YORK
text: I am thy king and thou a false-heart traitor.

speaker: YORK
text: Call hither to the stake my two brave bears,

speaker: YORK
text: That, with the very shaking of their chains,

speaker: YORK
text: They may astonish these fell-lurking curs.

speaker: YORK
text:
To
                 
                an
                 
                Attendant
                .
                 
              Bid Salisbury and Warwick come to me.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Are these thy bears? We’ll bait thy bears to death

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: And manacle the bearherd in their chains,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: If thou dar’st bring them to the baiting place.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Oft have I seen a hot o’erweening cur

speaker: RICHARD
text: Run back and bite because he was withheld,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Who, being suffered with the bear’s fell paw,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Hath clapped his tail between his legs and cried;

speaker: RICHARD
text: And such a piece of service will you do

speaker: RICHARD
text: If you oppose yourselves to match Lord Warwick.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Hence, heap of wrath, foul indigested lump,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: As crooked in thy manners as thy shape!

speaker: YORK
text: Nay, we shall heat you thoroughly anon.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Take heed, lest by your heat you burn yourselves.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Why, Warwick, hath thy knee forgot to bow?—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Old Salisbury, shame to thy silver hair,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Thou mad misleader of thy brainsick son!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: What, wilt thou on thy deathbed play the ruffian

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And seek for sorrow with thy spectacles?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: O, where is faith? O, where is loyalty?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: If it be banished from the frosty head,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Where shall it find a harbor in the earth?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Wilt thou go dig a grave to find out war,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And shame thine honorable age with blood?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Why art thou old and want’st experience?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Or wherefore dost abuse it, if thou hast it?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For shame! In duty bend thy knee to me

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That bows unto the grave with mickle age.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: My lord, I have considered with myself

speaker: SALISBURY
text: The title of this most renownèd duke,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: And in my conscience do repute his Grace

speaker: SALISBURY
text: The rightful heir to England’s royal seat.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Hast thou not sworn allegiance unto me?

speaker: SALISBURY
text: I have.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Canst thou dispense with heaven for such an oath?

speaker: SALISBURY
text: It is great sin to swear unto a sin,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: But greater sin to keep a sinful oath.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Who can be bound by any solemn vow

speaker: SALISBURY
text: To do a murd’rous deed, to rob a man,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: To force a spotless virgin’s chastity,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: To reave the orphan of his patrimony,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: To wring the widow from her customed right,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: And have no other reason for this wrong

speaker: SALISBURY
text: But that he was bound by a solemn oath?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: A subtle traitor needs no sophister.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Call Buckingham, and bid him arm himself.

speaker: YORK
text: Call Buckingham and all the friends thou hast,

speaker: YORK
text: I am resolved for death or dignity.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: The first, I warrant thee, if dreams prove true.

speaker: WARWICK
text: You were best to go to bed and dream again,

speaker: WARWICK
text: To keep thee from the tempest of the field.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: I am resolved to bear a greater storm

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Than any thou canst conjure up today;

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: And that I’ll write upon thy burgonet,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Might I but know thee by thy house’s badge.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Now, by my father’s badge, old Neville’s crest,

speaker: WARWICK
text: The rampant bear chained to the ragged staff,

speaker: WARWICK
text: This day I’ll wear aloft my burgonet—

speaker: WARWICK
text: As on a mountaintop the cedar shows

speaker: WARWICK
text: That keeps his leaves in spite of any storm—

speaker: WARWICK
text: Even to affright thee with the view thereof.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: And from thy burgonet I’ll rend thy bear

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: And tread it under foot with all contempt,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Despite the bearherd that protects the bear.

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: And so to arms, victorious father,

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: To quell the rebels and their complices.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Fie! Charity, for shame! Speak not in spite,

speaker: RICHARD
text: For you shall sup with Jesu Christ tonight.

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: Foul stigmatic, that’s more than thou canst tell!

speaker: RICHARD
text: If not in heaven, you’ll surely sup in hell.
